confirmation_budget.note: HEADLINE n_confirm = the drift-dominated band (build clearly off the bar): IID 405 → AR(1) 672 → measured-ACF 1,125 → flat 1,788. The GO-side confirm of a build genuinely in the thin [0.978,1.0] sliver is the variance-dominated case (14,915 IID / 41,379 measured-ACF at λ=1.0); the build-exactly-at-bar knife-edge peaks at 24,398 IID / 67,687 measured-ACF. The SPRT collapses the fixed-n 30,455 ~75× when drift-dominated (Deff-invariant).

confirm_go_at_anchor.regime: variance-dominated (dear; GO region is a thin sliver)
confirm_go_at_anchor.truth: build at full-recovery anchor λ=1.0 (only m=0.022 above the bar)

knife_edge_at_bar.truth: build exactly at the bar λ=μ_mid (indifference point)

feasibility.note: one served run = 65,536 q[2..9] positions. The HEADLINE drift-dominated budget 1,125 (measured-ACF) fits with 58× headroom; even the GO-confirm-at-anchor 41,379 fits. Only the measure-zero knife-edge (build EXACTLY at the bar, 67,687 measured-ACF) marginally exceeds one run (×1.03) — and #215's shallow-only λ̂≈0.9065 puts the build firmly in the drift-dominated regime, so gate-2 is measurable from the SAME launch-candidate run, no extra draw.

lcb_three_way_mapping.HOLD_kernel_feasible_below_bar: λ̂ ∈ [0.85715, 0.97798)  (a compliant int4 kernel is buildable per #216, but below the private-grade bar — HOLD, not GO)
lcb_three_way_mapping.NOGO_no_buildable_kernel: λ̂ < 0.85715  (no buildable kernel clears 500; deeper still, λ̂ < 0.83445 misses even with a FREE zero-overhead kernel, #213/#216) — NO-GO
lcb_three_way_mapping.PASS_go_eligible: λ̂_LCB ≥ 0.97798  (clears the binding private bar — gate-2 PASS)

gate2_decision_rule.note: the LCB read-out is coherent with the SPRT GO event: a build whose always-valid λ̂ lower-confidence-bound clears the bar is exactly the ΣZ≥A decision. The q[2..9] reads are dominated by the shallow rungs (depths 2–4 carry the bulk of the Neyman info, #205), which nearly every decode position reaches, so the effective read count ≈ the full 65,536.

stopping_boundaries.rule: accumulate ΣZ over q[2..9] reads; ΣZ≥A ⇒ GO-eligible, ΣZ≤B ⇒ NO-GO, else continue

gate2_test_definition.composite_null_caveat: The Wald boundaries A,B control type-I error POINTWISE at the null boundary μ0=bar (where the SPRT is calibrated). The null H0: λ_built<bar is COMPOSITE; in the Gaussian known-σ² MLR construction here the OC is monotone in λ, so sup_{λ<bar} P(decide GO)=P_{μ0}(decide GO)≤α — the worst-case false-GO is at λ→bar⁻ and the boundary value bounds the whole composite null (the test is STRICTLY more conservative further below the bar, where |drift| is larger). The residual approximations are (i) the Wald overshoot (boundaries ignore excess over A,B → realized α≈0.05, asymptotically exact), and (ii) the per-step read is treated as exactly Gaussian. For an EXACTLY-uniform, assumption-agnostic always-valid guarantee, the one-sided mSPRT / confidence sequence (mixture-LLR; Robbins; Johari et al. 2016; Waudby-Smith & Ramdas 2022; Fischer & Ramdas 2024) is the textbook-correct instrument and yields the SAME λ̂_LCB read-out; the Wald SPRT is its drift-dominated practical approximation, which is what prices the E[N] band here. This caveat changes the INSTRUMENT, not the bar, the (α,power) target, or the AR(1)-corrected budget.

hypotheses.H0_nogo: λ_built < 0.97798 (below the binding private bar — NO-GO; null boundary μ0)
hypotheses.kind: one-sided composite confirmation of an upper-bounded parameter (λ ≤ 1.0)

gate2_test_definition.regime_rationale: Recommend the Wald SPRT over a truth-independent fixed-n test: the deployed build is expected to sit CLEARLY on one side of the bar (#215's shallow-only λ̂≈0.9065 misses; a passing build clears with margin), so the sequential test stops ~75× earlier than the fixed-n worst case. The LCB decision rule (below) is the natural read-out: a build whose always-valid λ̂ lower-confidence-bound clears the bar is exactly the SPRT GO event. Fall back to the fixed-n n_fss only as the truth-independent ceiling (build genuinely at the bar).

test_statistic.per_step_read: x_i = μ_ref + Σ_{d=2..9}(a_d/∂E[T]∂λ)(â_{d,i}−q_d): the both-bugs q[2..9] Neyman-weighted λ-equivalent accept read on full-ladder decode step i (the #205 construction), x_i ~ N(λ_built, σ²)
test_statistic.wald_llr_increment: z_i = (m/σ²)(x_i − μ_mid); accumulate ΣZ, stop at A (GO) or B (NO-GO)

within_prompt_correlation.note: prompts independent; within-prompt reads autocorrelated (decaying ACF) → design-effect inflation on E[N] (#212)

handoff_lines.land_71_and_fern_185: gate-2 (measured λ̂_built ≥ 0.97798 both-bugs, q[2..9] direct) is confirmed by a Wald SPRT over the deployed tree's q[2..9] Neyman-weighted accept stream (boundaries A=+2.944/B=-2.944, (α,power)=(0.05,0.95)): AR(1)-corrected confirmation budget n_confirm = 1,125 decode steps (measured-ACF; AR(1) 672; IID floor 405; flat 1,788), which DOES fit one served run's ~65,536 q[2..9] positions — so gate-2 is measurable from the SAME launch-candidate run with the decision rule λ̂_LCB ≥ 0.97798 ⇒ PASS, [0.857,0.978) ⇒ HOLD (kernel-feasible, below private bar), < 0.857 ⇒ NO-GO; fern #185 reads gate-2 as a single PASS/HOLD/NO-GO flag. CAVEAT: confirming a build genuinely in the thin [0.978,1.0] GO sliver is the variance-dominated case (41,379 measured-ACF), still inside one run.

operating_characteristic.note: the (α,power) live on the Wald boundaries A,B (unchanged by the within-prompt correlation, which only rescales E[N] via Deff); realized (0.05,0.95) at the indifference edges, reproduced from #205/#212.

separation_cost.note: distinguishing the bar 0.97798 from the kernel floor 0.857 (δ=0.1208, WIDE) costs only 1,009 fixed-n IID trials; from the near-bar 0.97 (δ=0.0080, NARROW) it costs 231,334 — a 229× blow-up. The SPRT collapses each ~75× when drift-dominated; the gate-2 zone anchors μ1 at the full-recovery 1.0 (δ=m=0.022 → fixed-n 30,455), never a near-bar point.

synthesis.verdict: GATE-2 RUNBOOK BANKED. The confirmation procedure is a Wald SPRT (boundaries A=+2.944/B=-2.944, indifference zone [0.97798, 1.0]) on the deployed tree's both-bugs q[2..9] Neyman-weighted accept stream, realized (α,power)=(0.05,0.95). The AR(1)-corrected confirmation budget is n_confirm = 405 IID → 672 AR(1) → 1,125 measured-ACF (HEADLINE, data-grounded) → 1,788 flat, vs the truth-independent fixed-n ceiling 30,455 (~75× SPRT collapse). HONEST: that headline is the drift-dominated cost (build clearly off the bar); a GO-confirm of a build genuinely in the thin [0.978,1.0] sliver costs 41,379 measured-ACF, and the build-exactly-at-bar knife-edge peaks at 67,687. Separation cost: distinguishing the bar from the kernel floor 0.857 (wide) is 1,009 IID, from 0.97 (narrow) 231,334 — a 229× swing. Decision rule: λ̂_LCB ≥ 0.97798 ⇒ PASS; [0.857,0.978) ⇒ HOLD; < 0.857 ⇒ NO-GO. gate2_confirmable_in_one_run = True (headline fits with 58× headroom in one served run's 65,536 q[2..9] positions). NOT a launch.
